" Assumption 4: Authorization = F_BKPF_BUK ACTVT 03.
"   Basis to add to existing FI_DISPLAY role. Confirmed.

" Code based on confirmed assumptions:
SELECT lifnr FROM lfb1
  WHERE bukrs IN @s_bukrs
    AND sperr = space        " Assumption 1: active = not blocked
    AND lifnr IN @s_lifnr
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_active_vendors).

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you specify the output structure in TS for a complex ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS mein output structure definition sabse important hissa hai. Types definition explicitly likho: TYPES BEGIN OF ty_output, har field ka naam, type, aur purpose. Calculated fields bhi include karo — aging buckets, concatenated texts, formatted values. Currency reference fields bhi include karo — bina inke field catalog mein CFIELDNAME set nahi hoga properly. Multiple line types ka ALV — summary + detail — ke liye alag structures define karo. TS mein structure sample bhi do — jaise: 'Column 1: Sales Org (VKORG, 4 chars), Column 2: Customer Name (NAME1, 35 chars), Column 3: Net Value (NETWR, amount with currency).' Yeh clarity se field catalog automatically derived ho sakti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Output structure in TS is the most precise section — it defines every column the user will see. I document a TYPES definition with each field's technical name, data type, length, and description. For currency amounts, both the amount field and the currency key field are included even if the currency key column will be hidden. For calculated fields, I specify the calculation formula and resulting type. TS also documents field catalog settings: column header text, output width, do_sum flag, hotspot flag for clickable fields, and no_out for hidden reference columns. This level of detail allows a peer developer to build the exact same report from the TS alone without needing to reference the FS.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS review finding: output structure had amount fields but no currency key fields — field catalog could not set CFIELDNAME properly. Added WAERK and WAERS to the TYPE definition with NO_OUT = true flag noted in TS. Also added BUCKET_CURRENCY TYPE WAERS for aging buckets with mixed currencies. After TS update, developer built correct field catalog first time without any UAT finding on currency formatting.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Defining output structure in TS as just 'same as VBAK structure' or referencing a database table directly. This leads to SELECT * tendencies and includes unnecessary columns. Always define a custom TYPE with only the fields the ALV needs — this is the blueprint for both the internal table and the field catalog.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS output structure definition → TYPE specification
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_vendor_aging,
         lifnr       TYPE lfa1-lifnr,       " Vendor number
         name1       TYPE lfa1-name1,       " Vendor name
         bukrs       TYPE lfa1-bukrs,       " Company code
         waers       TYPE waers,            " Currency (hidden ref)
         bucket0_30  TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 0-30 days amount
         bucket31_60 TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 31-60 days
         bucket61_90 TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 61-90 days
         bucket91    TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " 90+ days
         total_open  TYPE bsik-dmbtr,       " Grand total
       END OF ty_vendor_aging.

" TS field catalog notes (for developer reference):
" BUCKET0_30: CFIELDNAME=WAERS, DO_SUM=true, coltext='0-30 Days'
" BUCKET31_60: CFIELDNAME=WAERS, DO_SUM=true, coltext='31-60 Days'
" WAERS: NO_OUT=true (hidden currency reference column)
" TOTAL_OPEN: DO_SUM=true, coltext='Total Open', emphasis=true

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you write test cases in a TS for an ALV report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: TS mein test cases generic nahi hone chahiye — 'positive test, negative test' itna kaafi nahi hai. Specific scenarios likho: Test 1: company code 1000, date range current month, 100 vendors — expected: 100 rows, aging buckets correct, grand total matches. Test 2: future date range — expected: no data, meaningful message. Test 3: very long date range — expected: row limit hit warning. Test 4: restricted user — expected: only authorized company codes visible. Test 5: background job via SM36 — expected: spool generated, job status Finished in SM37. Test 6: Excel export — expected: amounts as numbers not text, currency column present. Yeh specific test cases developer ko guideline dete hain aur tester ko checklist dete hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: TS test cases for ALV reports should be scenario-specific, not generic. I document at least eight categories: happy path with expected row count and sample output; empty result with the specific selection criteria that triggers it; large volume with data count close to the row limit plus the warning message expectation; boundary value for calculated fields — for aging, exactly 30 days and exactly 31 days in different buckets; restricted user showing only authorized data; background job execution with spool verification; Excel export with numeric amount verification; and layout variant save and restore. Each test case includes the input parameters, expected output, and the specific verification steps. This TS test section becomes the UAT checklist and can be directly reused.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: TS with specific test cases reduced UAT defects by 60% in one project. Tester had a checklist instead of guessing scenarios. Aging boundary test caught a bug — exactly 30-day items were being placed in 31-60 bucket due to <= vs < operator mismatch in calculation. TS boundary test caught it before UAT — developer fixed before even showing to business team.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing generic test cases like 'ensure all fields display correctly' or 'verify performance is acceptable.' These are not testable. TS test cases must have specific inputs and specific expected outputs so anyone — developer or tester — can execute and verify without ambiguity.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS Test Cases — ALV Vendor Aging Report

" TC001: Happy Path
" Input: BUKRS=1000, LIFNR=* (all vendors), KEYDT=today
" Expected: rows returned, bucket columns populated,
"           grand totals match sum of individual rows
" Verify: row counts match SE16 BSIK count for same criteria

" TC002: Empty Result
" Input: BUKRS=9999 (test company code with no data)
" Expected: Status bar 'No records found for company code 9999'
"           No ALV grid displayed

" TC003: Row Limit
" Input: BUKRS=1000, LIFNR=* (large vendor base), wide date
" Expected: Warning 'Data limited to 5000 rows'
"           Exactly 5000 rows in ALV

" TC004: Boundary Value — Aging Bucket
" Input: Create test BSIK record with BLDAT = today - 30
" Expected: Record in bucket 0-30 (not 31-60)
" Input: BLDAT = today - 31
" Expected: Record in bucket 31-60

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you handle the FS to TS conversion when the requirement involves HANA-specific optimization?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: S/4HANA project mein FS to TS conversion mein HANA optimization explicitly specify karna padta hai. FS mein hoga: 'Report bahut fast run honi chahiye, large data.' TS mein HANA-specific decisions: CDS Analytical Query use karo agar pure read-only analytical report hai — yeh HANA pe best performance deta hai. AMDP use karo complex calculations ke liye jo ABAP mein slow honge. HANA CASE expressions in SELECT use karo calculated columns ke liye — ek hi query mein multiple bucket calculations. Agar OO ALV ya REUSE ALV use karna hai — SALV CL_SALV_TABLE recommend karo — yeh modern approach hai REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY se beter. TS mein explicitly bolo: 'S/4HANA simplification list check karo — BSEG ki jagah ACDOCA use karo financial data ke liye.'
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For S/4HANA projects, the TS must explicitly address HANA optimization opportunities. I specify: use ACDOCA instead of BSEG for financial line items — this follows S/4HANA simplification list guidance; use HANA native CASE expressions in SELECT for calculated columns like aging buckets — pushes computation to database layer; consider CDS Analytical Query annotated with @Analytics.query: true for pure read-only analytical reports — these benefit most from HANA columnar storage; use CL_SALV_TABLE instead of the older REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY function module for cleaner code. The TS also documents any performance testing baseline — existing report on ECC versus target on S/4HANA — so go-live performance gain can be demonstrated.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: S/4HANA migration TS decision: aging report was using BSEG on ECC — 3 minutes for 500k items. TS specified ACDOCA migration with HANA aggregation. After migration: same report 8 seconds. TS documented expected performance improvement as 'target under 30 seconds' based on HANA benchmark data. Client was very impressed with the demonstrated improvement — it justified the migration cost.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Writing an S/4HANA TS that still specifies old ECC table BSEG for financial data. Developer implements as specified, performance is no better than ECC, migration value is not realized. TS must explicitly name S/4HANA replacement tables and HANA-optimized approaches — do not assume developers know the S/4HANA changes without being told in the TS.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" TS HANA optimization specification → code
" TS says: Use ACDOCA, push aggregation to HANA

" S/4HANA optimized: ACDOCA + HANA aggregation + CASE expression
SELECT rbukrs AS bukrs,
       racct  AS hkont,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - budat ) <= 30
              THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END ) AS bucket0_30,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - budat ) BETWEEN 31 AND 60
              THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END ) AS bucket31_60,
       SUM( CASE WHEN ( @sy-datum - budat ) > 60
              THEN dmbtr ELSE 0 END ) AS bucket61plus,
       SUM( dmbtr ) AS total_open
  FROM acdoca                         " S/4HANA: not BSEG
  WHERE rbukrs IN @s_bukrs
    AND rldnr   = '0L'               " Leading ledger
    AND augdt   = '00000000'         " Open items only
  GROUP BY rbukrs, racct
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_aging_hana)
  UP TO 10000 ROWS.
